---
title: "LLM inference — GPT-4o-mini (OpenAI, parallel, row-level resume)"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# What this file is

A **standalone runner** for **GPT-4o-mini (OpenAI)** — one model, so it never blocks behind the others. It reads the shared data (`../artifacts/gossip_model_data.csv`) and the frozen exemplar IDs (`../artifacts/llm_fewshot_exemplars_ids.csv`), reconstructs the exemplar text from the train split, and rebuilds the **identical** prompts every model uses, so rows and text are byte-for-byte the same. It writes:

- `../artifacts/llm_preds_gpt4o_mini_zero.csv`
- `../artifacts/llm_preds_gpt4o_mini_few.csv`

When both exist, run [`04b_llm_eval.qmd`](04b_llm_eval.qmd) (it picks them up by filename).

> **How to run.** Open in RStudio and **Run All Chunks** (Ctrl+Alt+R); do not `quarto render`. GPT-4o-mini is queried with live parallel requests (`parallel_chat_text()`, temperature 0). The run is **row-level resumable**: each answer is appended to a partial file the moment it arrives, every run requests only the rows still missing, and two consecutive empty waves (a rate limit) stop it cleanly — so a dropped connection or closed laptop costs nothing; just run it again. Throughput is bound by your OpenAI usage tier; on Tier 2+ raise `MAX_ACTIVE` in the run chunk.

# Setup: libraries

```{r v191-libs}
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# Standalone session: load everything this file needs on its own.
pkgs <- c("tidyverse", "ellmer", "withr")
missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())]
if (length(missing) > 0) install.packages(missing)
invisible(lapply(pkgs, library, character.only = TRUE))

cat("Loaded:", paste(pkgs, collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

# Shared data (same source of truth as every other model)

```{r v191-data}
#| message: true
#| warning: false

# ── Read the single shared modelling table; rebuild the exact test/train frames ─────────
model_data_path <- "../artifacts/gossip_model_data.csv"
stopifnot(file.exists(model_data_path))

model_data <- readr::read_csv(model_data_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    subclass = factor(subclass, levels = c("HF", "MF", "HR", "MR")),
    label    = factor(label,    levels = c("fake", "real")),
    split    = factor(split,    levels = c("train", "validation", "test"))
  )

test_df  <- model_data %>% filter(split == "test")    # 2,796 rows — scored by all models
train_df <- model_data %>% filter(split == "train")   # 60% — few-shot exemplars come from here

cat("Loaded", nrow(model_data), "rows  |  test =", nrow(test_df),
    " train =", nrow(train_df), "\n")
```

# The 8 frozen few-shot exemplars (identical to the main doc)

```{r v191-exemplars}
#| message: true
#| warning: false

llm_test <- test_df %>% select(doc_id, subclass, label, doc_text)
stopifnot(nrow(llm_test) == 2796)

# Frozen 8 few-shot exemplars (2 per subclass). Only doc_id+subclass+label are
# committed (../artifacts/llm_fewshot_exemplars_ids.csv) — no article text on
# GitHub — and the text is reconstructed by joining those IDs to train_df. If the
# IDs file is absent, the identical 8 are regenerated from the fixed seed.
exemplar_ids_path <- "../artifacts/llm_fewshot_exemplars_ids.csv"
if (file.exists(exemplar_ids_path)) {
  exemplar_ids <- readr::read_csv(exemplar_ids_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  set.seed(2026)
  exemplar_ids <- train_df %>%
    group_by(subclass) %>%
    slice_sample(n = 2) %>%
    mutate(rep = row_number()) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    arrange(rep, match(subclass, c("HF", "HR", "MF", "MR"))) %>%
    transmute(doc_id, subclass = as.character(subclass), label = as.character(label))
  readr::write_csv(exemplar_ids, exemplar_ids_path)
}

# Recover exemplar text from train_df by doc_id, preserving the frozen order.
exemplars <- exemplar_ids %>%
  mutate(subclass = factor(subclass, levels = c("HF", "MF", "HR", "MR")),
         label    = as.character(label)) %>%
  left_join(train_df %>% select(doc_id, doc_text), by = "doc_id")
stopifnot(!any(is.na(exemplars$doc_text)))   # every exemplar id must be in train_df

stopifnot(nrow(exemplars) == 8)
stopifnot(length(intersect(exemplars$doc_id, llm_test$doc_id)) == 0)   # no leakage
cat("Exemplar order (label):", paste(exemplars$label, collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

# Prompts, model registry, keys

```{r v191-prompts}
#| message: true
#| warning: false

sys_base <- paste(
  "Read the following news article and determine whether it is real or fake",
  "Answer with exactly one word, lowercase, with no punctuation and no explanation:",
  "either fake or real.",
  sep = "\n"
)

render_exemplars <- function(ex) {
  blocks <- purrr::pmap_chr(
    ex[c("doc_text", "label")],
    function(doc_text, label) paste0("Article:\n", doc_text, "\n\nAnswer: ", label)
  )
  paste0(
    "Here are labelled examples:\n\n",
    paste(blocks, collapse = "\n\n---\n\n"),
    "\n\nNow classify the next article in the same way."
  )
}

sys_zero <- sys_base
sys_few  <- paste(sys_base, render_exemplars(exemplars), sep = "\n\n")

test_prompts <- as.list(paste0("Article:\n", llm_test$doc_text))
stopifnot(length(test_prompts) == nrow(llm_test))

models_spec <- tibble::tribble(
  ~key,           ~provider,    ~model,                      ~method,
  "gpt4o_mini",   "openai",     "gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18",     "parallel",
  "claude_haiku", "anthropic",  "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",  "batch",
  "gemini_flash", "gemini",     "gemini-2.5-flash",           "parallel"
)

load_api_keys <- function(path = "API Keys.txt") {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    stop("API key file not found: ", normalizePath(path, mustWork = FALSE),
         "\n  -> create 'API Keys.txt' with lines OPENAI_API_KEY=, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=, GEMINI_API_KEY=")
  }
  lines <- trimws(readLines(path, warn = FALSE))
  lines <- lines[nzchar(lines) & !startsWith(lines, "#") & grepl("=", lines, fixed = TRUE)]
  env <- setNames(trimws(sub("^[^=]*=", "", lines)),
                  trimws(sub("=.*$", "", lines)))
  keys <- list(
    openai    = unname(env["OPENAI_API_KEY"]),
    anthropic = unname(env["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"]),
    gemini    = unname(env["GEMINI_API_KEY"])
  )
  miss <- names(keys)[vapply(keys, function(k) is.na(k) || !nzchar(k), logical(1))]
  if (length(miss) > 0) {
    stop("API Keys.txt is missing/blank for: ", paste(miss, collapse = ", "),
         " (expected OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY)")
  }
  keys
}

# Temperature 0 for reproducibility. The large 8-shot system prompt is sent on
# every call, but OpenAI auto-caches prompts >1024 tokens, so after the first
# request the repeated exemplars are billed at the cached (cheaper) rate.
make_chat <- function(provider, model, system_prompt, keys) {
  switch(provider,
    openai    = chat_openai(model = model, system_prompt = system_prompt,
                            credentials = function() keys$openai,    params = params(temperature = 0)),
    anthropic = chat_anthropic(model = model, system_prompt = system_prompt,
                               credentials = function() keys$anthropic, params = params(temperature = 0)),
    gemini    = chat_google_gemini(model = model, system_prompt = system_prompt,
                                   credentials = function() keys$gemini, params = params(temperature = 0)),
    stop("unknown provider: ", provider)
  )
}

write_preds <- function(key, setting_name, raw) {
  out_csv <- sprintf("../artifacts/llm_preds_%s_%s.csv", key, setting_name)
  stopifnot(length(raw) == nrow(llm_test))             # positional alignment
  tibble(
    doc_id   = llm_test$doc_id,
    subclass = as.character(llm_test$subclass),
    truth    = as.character(llm_test$label),
    setting  = setting_name,
    model    = key,
    raw      = raw
  ) %>% readr::write_csv(out_csv)
  message("✓ wrote ", out_csv, "  (", length(raw), " rows)")
}
```

# Run GPT-4o-mini (zero-shot and 8-shot) — row-level resumable

```{r v191-run}
#| message: true
#| warning: false

api_keys <- load_api_keys()                  # errors early if the key file is missing
this_key <- "gpt4o_mini"
spec     <- models_spec %>% filter(key == this_key)
settings <- list(zero = sys_zero, few = sys_few)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# TUNING  (the only two knobs you should touch)
#   max_active : how many requests run at once.
#                Tier 1     -> 8–10 is safe (gpt-4o-mini is 200k tokens/min, and
#                              the 8-shot prompts are large, so don't over-shoot).
#                Tier 2+    -> raise to 40–100 for big speed-ups.
#   batch_size : rows requested per wave; results are saved after EVERY wave, so
#                smaller = more frequent checkpoints (and more visible progress).
MAX_ACTIVE <- 10
BATCH_SIZE <- 25
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Run one regime. Keeps a partial CSV of successful (doc_id, raw) rows; each run
# requests ONLY the rows still missing and appends new successes immediately. If
# two waves in a row come back completely empty, you are rate-limited / out of
# quota, so it stops cleanly (re-run later — progress is saved).
run_openai_setting <- function(setting_name, max_active = MAX_ACTIVE, batch_size = BATCH_SIZE) {
  out_csv <- sprintf("../artifacts/llm_preds_%s_%s.csv", this_key, setting_name)
  if (file.exists(out_csv)) {
    message("✓ already complete, skipping: ", out_csv); return(invisible())
  }

  chat        <- make_chat(spec$provider, spec$model, settings[[setting_name]], api_keys)
  partial_csv <- sprintf(".llm_%s_%s_partial.csv", this_key, setting_name)

  all_ids       <- llm_test$doc_id
  prompts_by_id <- setNames(test_prompts, all_ids)        # doc_id -> prompt

  # What did earlier runs already finish?
  done <- tibble(doc_id = character(), raw = character())
  if (file.exists(partial_csv)) {
    done <- readr::read_csv(partial_csv, show_col_types = FALSE,
                            col_types = readr::cols(.default = "c")) %>%
            filter(!is.na(raw)) %>% distinct(doc_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
  }
  todo_ids <- setdiff(all_ids, done$doc_id)
  message(sprintf(">> %s / %s : %d already done, %d to go (max_active=%d, batch=%d)",
                  this_key, setting_name, nrow(done), length(todo_ids), max_active, batch_size))

  if (length(todo_ids) > 0) {
    waves <- split(todo_ids, ceiling(seq_along(todo_ids) / batch_size))
    consecutive_fail <- 0L
    for (w in seq_along(waves)) {
      ids <- waves[[w]]
      res <- parallel_chat_text(chat, unname(prompts_by_id[ids]),
                                max_active = max_active, on_error = "continue")
      ok  <- !is.na(res)
      if (any(ok)) {
        readr::write_csv(tibble(doc_id = ids[ok], raw = res[ok]),
                         partial_csv, append = file.exists(partial_csv))
        done <- bind_rows(done, tibble(doc_id = ids[ok], raw = res[ok]))
      }
      message(sprintf("   wave %d/%d: +%d ok, %d failed  |  total %d/%d",
                      w, length(waves), sum(ok), sum(!ok), nrow(done), length(all_ids)))

      consecutive_fail <- if (sum(ok) == 0) consecutive_fail + 1L else 0L
      if (consecutive_fail >= 2) {
        message("   ⏸ Two waves failed in a row — you are rate-limited (RPM/TPM) or out of quota.\n",
                "      Progress is SAVED in ", partial_csv, ". Lower MAX_ACTIVE and/or re-run this\n",
                "      file later to continue; raising your OpenAI usage tier removes the limit.")
        return(invisible())
      }
    }
  }

  # Complete? Assemble in the original test order and write the final CSV.
  final     <- tibble(doc_id = all_ids) %>% left_join(done, by = "doc_id")
  n_missing <- sum(is.na(final$raw))
  if (n_missing > 0) {
    message(sprintf("   still missing %d row(s) — re-run later to finish (progress saved in %s).",
                    n_missing, partial_csv))
    return(invisible())
  }
  write_preds(this_key, setting_name, final$raw)
  message("   complete — you can delete ", partial_csv, " now if you like.")
}

for (s in names(settings)) run_openai_setting(s)

message("\nGPT-4o-mini pass finished. If a regime stopped early, just run this file again later.\n",
        "When llm_preds_gpt4o_mini_zero.csv AND llm_preds_gpt4o_mini_few.csv both exist in ../artifacts,\n",
        "run 04a_llm_infer_anthropic.qmd and 04a_llm_infer_gemini.qmd, then analysis/04b_llm_eval.qmd.")
```
